compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Why
The diff-scope filter keeps a detector match only when `location.file` is EXACTLY equal to an
entry in `changed_files` (`src/rebar/llm/code_review/detectors.py:74`, `if loc and loc in
changed:`), with neither side normalized. A location and a changed-file entry that denote the
SAME file with different spellings (`./app.py`, an absolute path under repo_root, `a//b.py`
repeated separators, a unicode NFC/NFD variant) compare unequal, so a real high-severity
security match is silently dropped. VALIDATED.

## Decision: normalize BOTH sides at this boundary
Add a small canonicalizer applied to `location.file` AND each `changed_files` entry before the
membership test — a pure textual canonical form (no filesystem access, since diff-scope paths
need not exist on disk):
1. If the path is absolute AND under `repo_root`, make it relative to `repo_root` (both sides
   then live in the same relative space; the upstream contract already intends repo-relative).
2. `os.path.normpath` — collapses `./`, repeated separators (`a//b` → `a/b`), and `..`.
3. `unicodedata.normalize("NFC", …)` — so a composed/decomposed unicode filename matches.
Symlink resolution (realpath) is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE: it requires the file to exist on
disk, which a diff-scope path need not; this ticket canonicalizes spelling, not inode identity
(noted in a code comment).

## What
1. `detectors.py`: add `_canonical_path(p, repo_root)` (relativize-under-repo_root → normpath →
   NFC) and apply it to both `loc` and every `changed_files` entry (build the `changed` set from
   canonicalized entries; canonicalize `loc` before the `in` test).
2. `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws5.py`: variant-path matching tests (RED-first — they fail
   against the current exact-match code, pass after canonicalization).

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/detectors.py` — canonicalize both sides of the diff-scope filter.
- `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws5.py` — variant-path-form tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `location.file` and `changed_files` are both canonicalized (relativize-under-repo_root +
      normpath + NFC) before the membership test.
- [ ] Tests: a match whose `location.file` is `./app.py`, an absolute path under repo_root,
      `a//b.py` (repeated separators), and an NFC/NFD unicode variant each STILL matches its
      changed-file entry `app.py` / `a/b.py` / the unicode name — the high-severity match is
      retained.
- [ ] A genuinely-different file (e.g. `other.py` not in `changed_files`) still does NOT match.
- [ ] `make test` green.

file_impact.reason: normalize both sides of diff-scope path membership (or assert upstream contract)
path-variant match tests (./, absolute, repeated sep, unicode)

compiled_state.title: Define and test the security-detector diff-scope path contract
